headline: Hedge Funds Pivot to Bearish Dollar Bets on US-Iran Talks Optimism
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Hedge funds are increasingly downbeat on the dollar as the prospect of a two-week ceasefire extension between the US and Iran sap the currency’s war-driven strength. Investors added to their bearish dollar trades this month through April 10, based on a proprietary trading model from Morgan Stanley.In the options market, so-called risk reversals on the Bloomberg dollar index show the premium to hedge against a stronger dollar compared with bets against a weaker greenback has narrow

headline: CoreWeave Taps US Junk-Debt Market Again With AI Bond Reopening
summary: (Bloomberg) -- CoreWeave Inc. is tapping the US junk-bond market for a second time in a week as the cloud infrastructure firm seizes on strong investor demand.The Livingston, New Jersey-based company is selling $1 billion of additional 9.75% senior notes due 2031, it said in a statement. Proceeds will repay outstanding debt among other corporate uses, it added. The debt is being pitched at a price of 101.5 to 102 cents on dollar, and may be sold as soon as Thursday, according to a person with kn

headline: CoreWeave Taps US Junk-Debt Market Again With Bond Reopening
summary: (Bloomberg) -- CoreWeave Inc. tapped the US junk-bond market for a second time in a week as the cloud infrastructure firm seizes on strong investor demand for AI-related debt. The Livingston, New Jersey-based company sold $1 billion of additional 9.75% senior notes due 2031, according to a person with direct knowledge of the matter. The debt priced at 102 cents on dollar, the person added, asking not to be identified because details are private. The offering was run by JPMorgan Chase & Co., Morg

The Livingston, New Jersey-based company sold $1 billion of additional 9.75% senior notes due 2031, according to a person with direct knowledge of the matter. headline: Corning Stock Is Falling. Why J.P. Morgan Disagrees With the ‘Blue-Sky Scenario.’
summary: Shares of  Corning  declined Thursday after  J.P. Morgan  analysts downgraded the artificial-intelligence beneficiary with the firm cautious heading into earnings.  A J.P. Morgan analyst team, led by Samik Chatterjee, downgraded  Corning  to Neutral from Overweight.  The firm, however, raised its price target on Corning stock to $175 from $115.

content.title: Corning Stock Is Falling. Why J.P. Morgan Disagrees With the ‘Blue-Sky Scenario.’
content.summary: Shares of  Corning  declined Thursday after  J.P. Morgan  analysts downgraded the artificial-intelligence beneficiary with the firm cautious heading into earnings.  A J.P. Morgan analyst team, led by Samik Chatterjee, downgraded  Corning  to Neutral from Overweight.  The firm, however, raised its price target on Corning stock to $175 from $115.

headline: Abbott Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Stock Is Slumping.
summary: Abbott Laboratories  posted first-quarter earnings that edged past analysts’ estimates but a protracted slump in one business line and a dilutive acquisition sent shares tumbling on Thursday.  Abbott reported adjusted earnings of $1.15 a share, beating Wall Street’s calls for $1.14.  Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.

content.title: Abbott Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Stock Is Slumping.
content.summary: Abbott Laboratories  posted first-quarter earnings that edged past analysts’ estimates but a protracted slump in one business line and a dilutive acquisition sent shares tumbling on Thursday.  Abbott reported adjusted earnings of $1.15 a share, beating Wall Street’s calls for $1.14.  Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.

headline: 3 Dividend Aristocrat ETFs to Buy Before 2026 Markets Shift

headline: Big Bank earnings: How top CEOs feel about private credit risks
summary: Big Banks JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), Bank of America (BAC), and Citigroup (C) kicked off earnings season by reporting their respective quarterly results throughout this week. What did the performance of these institutions indicate about the state of the banking sector? Yahoo Finance Senior Banking Reporter David Hollerith breaks down the biggest takeaways from these major banks' earnings and their chief executives' commentary.

content.title: Big Bank earnings: How top CEOs feel about private credit risks
content.description:
Big Banks JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Goldman Sachs (GS), Morgan Stanley (MS), Bank of America (BAC), and Citigroup (C) kicked off earnings season by reporting their respective quarterly results throughout this week.

What did the performance of these institutions indicate about the state of the banking sector?

Yahoo Finance Senior Banking Reporter David Hollerith breaks down the biggest takeaways from these major banks' earnings and their chief executives' commentary.

What did the performance of these institutions indicate about the state of the banking sector? Yahoo Finance Senior Banking Reporter David Hollerith breaks down the biggest takeaways from these major banks' earnings and their chief executives' commentary.

headline: Abbott Earnings Beat Estimates—but It Was All Downhill From There
summary: Abbott Laboratories  posted first-quarter earnings that edged past analysts’ estimates but a protracted slump in one business line and a dilutive acquisition sent shares tumbling on Thursday.  Abbott reported adjusted earnings of $1.15 a share, beating Wall Street’s calls for $1.14.  Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.

content.title: Abbott Earnings Beat Estimates—but It Was All Downhill From There
content.summary: Abbott Laboratories  posted first-quarter earnings that edged past analysts’ estimates but a protracted slump in one business line and a dilutive acquisition sent shares tumbling on Thursday.  Abbott reported adjusted earnings of $1.15 a share, beating Wall Street’s calls for $1.14.  Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.
